sections.paragraph:
προσδέομαι (Hdt. et al.) pass. dep. fut. 3 sg. προσδεηθήσεται (Sir 13:3); 1 aor. 3 sg. προσεδεήθη (Sir 42:21) orig. ‘need in addition / further’ (so Thu. et al.; ins; pap; Pr 12:9; Sir; Jos., Ant. 7, 340; Ar. 10, 2—but the force of the προς is no longer felt e.g. in Epict. 1, 16, 1; SIG 313, 11 [IV b.c.]; UPZ 110, 154 [164 b.c.]; PTebt 59, 8 [99 b.c.]) to have need of someth., need, w. gen. of what is needed (Thu. 2, 41, 4 al.; EpArist 11; 113) Dg 3:4a. Of God, who has need of nothing 3:5; Ac 17:25 (s. New Docs 4, 86 for the view that the force of πρός may be felt here: ‘God needs nothing [beyond what he has] from anyone … ’.); cp. Dg 3:3, 4b (a Gr-Rom. commonplace: Pla., Tim. 34b; Aristot., Eth. Eud. 7, 12; ‘Onatas’ the Pythagorean in Stob., Ecl. 1, 1, 39 vol. I 49, 20 W. [Norden, Agn. Th. 14]; Philo, Op. M. 13; 46; cp. Eur., Herc. Furens 1345f: H. asks, δεῖται γὰρ ὁ θεὸς, εἴπερ ἔστʼ ὀρθῶς θεός, οὐδενός;=Is God, if he is indeed God, in need of anything?).—DELG s.v. 2 δέω. M-M. TW.

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

dep. dep. = deponent

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

aor. aor. = aorist

orig. orig. = original(ly)

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

someth. someth. = something

gen. gen. = genitive

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Gr-Rom. Gr-Rom. = Greco-Roman (gener. in contrast to Israelite/Christian tradition)

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Stob Stob , V a.d.—List 5

vol. vol. = volume

Norden, Agn. Th. Norden, Agn. Th. = EN., Agnostos Theos—List 6

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 876
